english: ‚dra ('dra) just as the water of the ditch makes the fields green Med.; sá-bon (sa bon) Dzl. (v. be-fore, but it may as well be referred to this signification); gsos skyéd-pa (gsos skyed pa) to bring up, to nurse up Dzl.; skyed sríṅ-ba (skyed sring ba) id. Glr. - 4. = skyé-ba (skye ba), to bring on, carry, convey to a place Pth. Comp. skyed-mos-tsál (skyed mos tshal) grove, park. - skyed-rdzógs (skyed rdzogs), instead of skyed-rím (skyed rim) and rdzogs-rím (rdzogs rim), उत्पन्नक्रम and सम्पन्नक्रम, two kinds or degrees of meditation. - skyed-śiṅ (skyed shing) Cs.: a planted tree (?) prob. a fruit-tree, Dzl. II sbst. 1. the generating, producing etc. - 2. = skyed (skyed), e. g. skyéd-pa lén-pa (skyed pa len pa) to gain flesh, to thrive C. - 3. = rkéd-pa (rked pa).

adj. 1. quick, swift Lex., khró- (khro-) or sdaṅ-skyen-pa (sdang skyen pa) quick to wrath Stg.; byéd-skyen-pa (byed skyen pa) rash, hasty, precipitate Glr. - 2. nimble, dexterous C. W.; ‚póṅ-skyen-pa ('phong skyen pa) dexterous in shooting, a skilful archer Dzl. (Besides: vb. to make haste, to strive; sbst. zeal, ardour; adj. strong Cs., Sch. ??)

resp. 1. thirst. - 2. drink, beverage, esp. tea, also źal-skyéms (zhal skyems) or -skyoms (-skyoms), skyems ‚drén pa (skyems 'dren pa) to offer or set before an honoured person something to drink, bźés-pa (bzhes pa) to accept of it, to take it; skyems-la gsol-rés byéd-pa (skyems la gsol res byed pa) to drink beer in company Glr.; gśegs-skyéms (gshegs skyems) a carousal on the departure of an honoured person; gser-skyéms (gser skyems) beer together with grains of corn, as an offering to the gods for the good success of an enterprise, a journey etc., in religious dancing-festivals, gser-skyéms-pa (gser skyems pa) sbst. the priest or dancer who offers it. - skyems-ćaṅ (skyems chang) beer. - skyems-ćú (skyems chu) drinkable water. - *skyéms-daṅ* (*skyems dang*) W. (?) brandy. - skyéms-tsúgs (skyems tshugs) Sch.: cup, dish. - skyéms-siṅ (skyems sing) small-beer.
Lex.: curcuma, turmeric; in W. barberry.
vulgo for ske-dmán (ske dman) woman C. (v. skye-ba (skye ba) II).

pf. & ft. bskyel (bskyel), imp. skyol (skyol) 1. to conduct, accompany, resp. gdan-skyél-ba (gdan skyel ba); skyól-la śog (skyol la shog) conduct him hither! Pth.; *skyel-la-la* (*skyel la la*) (for *skyél-wa-la* (*skyel wa la*)) soṅ (song) he has gone to accompany (him) W. - bsu-bskyál (bsu bskyal) going to meet, and accompanying on departing Dzl., gśegs-skyél byéd-pa (gshegs skyel byed pa) resp. to accompany an honoured person on departing, to see him off Mil. - 2. to convey, bring, take e. g. a child to a place, food to somebody, Dzl., C. W. id.; to carry off, to take away C.: *śiṅ ma kyal ćig* (*shing ma kyal cig*) do not bring any more wood! more accurately *kyal śog* (*kyal shog*) bring! *kyal soṅ* (*kyal song*) take away! - 3. to send B. & C. e.g. clothes to somebody Dzl. - 4. to risk, to stake, raṅ-srog (rang srog) Mil. - 5. C.: to use, to employ *bá-laṅ le jhé'-pa-la* (*ba lang las byed pa la*) an ox for work; to spend, *le jhé'-pa-la mí-tse* (*las byed pa la mi tshe*) one's whole life in working, *lé-lo náṅ-na* (*le lo nang na*) in idleness. - 6. *ḱa kyél-wa* (*kha skyel wa*) C. to kiss; gnód-pa skyél-ba (gnod pa skyel ba), B., *kyal-wa* (*kyal wa*) C. W. col., to do harm, to hurt, inflict an injury, to play one a trick; mna skyel-ba (mna' skyel ba) B., C. W., to swear, take an oath; *lo kyél-će* (*blo skyel che*) W. to rel., depend upon, confide in. - skyel-tuṅ byéd-pa (skyel thung byed pa) = gśegs-skyél byéd-pa (gshegs skyel byed pa), (prop.: to accompany one to a short distance). - skyel-bdár (skyel bdar) Lex., also col., present of the departing person to those that accompany him. - skyel-ma (skyel ma) an escort, convoy; skyél-mar yod (skyel mar yod) he is a guide (to me) Mil.; skyél-ma źu (skyel ma zhu) we ask for a safe-conduct Glr.; dmag daṅ bcás-pai skyél-ma (dmag dang bcas pa'i skyel ma) a military escort Glr.

also skyás-ma (skyas ma), skyós-ma (skyos ma), kyós-ma (kyos ma), resp. gnaṅ-skyés (gnang skyes), a present, skyes skúr-ba (skyes skur ba) to give or send a present; ‚byon-skyés ('byon skyes), phebs-skyes (phebs skyes) a present given to or received from somebody on his arrival. - skyas-ćaṅ (skyas chang) a present of beer, skyes-kúr (skyes kur) of cakes, skyes-nór (skyes nor) of merchandise or money; skyes-lán (skyes lan) a present made in return Cs.
1. pt. pf. of skyé-ba (skye ba). - 2. sbst. man, male person, skyés-pa (skyes pa)
